note: Legacy manual/semimanual mask_top_shortcut kept as the old-method control.
Must be located dynamically from TCP data offset. Do not use a fixed offset when the TCP header is short, because bytes after the TCP header are payload in Pcap-Encoder and padding in ShallowML.
TCP flags and window size can reflect packet role, OS defaults, application stack, and trace construction.
IPv4 basic header fields. total_length can be legitimate packet-size signal or dataset artifact; TTL/protocol/IP ID/checksum are higher shortcut-risk fields.
Column 4 in common IPv4/IHL=5 packets. TTL is strongly tied to OS/network path/capture environment, while protocol is a coarse transport indicator.
Length is mixed evidence; IP ID/checksum can reflect stack/capture artifacts and packet construction.
Sanity-check baseline: randomly selected non-shortcut columns with the same cardinality as current_mask_top_shortcut.
Seq/Ack can encode implicit flow identity in packet-level splits. Ports are already zeroed, but sequence space may still leak flow/session structure.
Only applies to UDP packets. On TCP-only subsets this group should be skipped in pcap-aware masking; default columns are only for lightweight CSV scoring.
Checksum can be an artifact after IP/port normalization; urgent pointer is usually zero but still included for field-complete TCP header masking.
